behaviors.description: A file placed inside a subdirectory of the watched root is processed, not only files placed directly in the root directory.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is real filesystem watcher recursion/event delivery. MOCK-MISS: this is not just local parsing logic; it depends on watcher behavior across directory boundaries. REAL-INFRA and MOCK-HIDE: mocked events can hide OS-specific non-recursive watch misconfiguration. Rejected higher levels: behavior text contains no AI/ML decision stage, so Agentic/Workflow are disqualified.
behaviors.test_description: Start the daemon against a real temp directory tree, create `root/sub/a.txt`, and assert exactly one processed output appears for that file path without restarting the process.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: A lower-level test with synthetic events would miss real recursive-watch failures.

behaviors.description: The deduplication logic correctly discards a second processing request for the same file path within a configurable time window.
behaviors.justification: Minimum failure surface is module handoff between request intake and dedup state/timer logic. MOCK-MISS: can be caught by wiring real in-process components with controlled time; no mandatory OS/network/subprocess needed. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: no real AI/ML output and no multi-agent sequence. Rejected higher level: behavior says 'deduplication logic...within a configurable time window' and does not require real filesystem semantics.
behaviors.test_description: Wire intake + dedup + dispatcher in-process with a fake/controllable clock, submit same path twice inside the window, assert first is dispatched and second is dropped; then advance past window and assert dispatch occurs again.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: This targets cross-module state/time interaction; unit of a single helper would be too narrow.

behaviors.description: When the operating system emits multiple filesystem events for a single file write, the daemon processes the file exactly once.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly depends on OS event duplication. REAL-INFRA: requires real watcher/backend behavior. MOCK-HIDE: synthetic single-event mocks often pass while real backends emit burst events. Rejected higher levels: no AI/ML content quality or multi-agent chain in behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: Run daemon on a real watched directory, perform a write pattern known to trigger multiple events (e.g., temp-write+rename), and assert one processing action/output for that file.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Only real OS event streams exercise this failure mode.

behaviors.description: If the watched directory is removed and then recreated while the daemon is running, the daemon resumes watching the new directory without a restart.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is OS filesystem lifecycle events plus long-running watcher recovery. REAL-INFRA and MOCK-HIDE: in-process fakes rarely model watcher invalidation/rebind semantics after directory deletion/recreation. Rejected higher levels: no AI/ML decision dependency or multi-step agent pipeline in behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: Start daemon on a real directory, delete the watched directory, recreate it at the same path, then add a new file and assert it is processed without restarting daemon.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Integration stubs cannot reliably surface backend watcher re-registration failures.

behaviors.description: When multiple files arrive at the same time, they are processed in the order determined by their creation timestamps.
behaviors.justification: Ordering correctness depends on real filesystem timestamp characteristics and concurrent arrival events, not just queue sorting logic. MOCK-HIDE: mocked timestamps/events can hide granularity/tie behavior seen on real FS backends. Rejected higher levels: behavior text is infrastructure ordering, not AI/ML judgment.
behaviors.test_description: Create multiple files nearly simultaneously in the real watched directory with controlled creation times, then assert processing order matches observed filesystem creation timestamp ordering (including deterministic tie-break rule if defined).
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: A pure in-memory test can validate sorter logic but not real timestamp/event interaction failures.

behaviors.description: A file that is still being written to when the arrival event fires is not picked up for processing until writing is complete.
behaviors.justification: Failure appears at real write/event timing boundary (partial writes, rename/close semantics). REAL-INFRA: requires actual filesystem write progression. MOCK-HIDE: mocks generally deliver atomic completion and miss partial-read races. Rejected higher levels: no AI/ML output dependence or agentic sequence in behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: Open a file in the watched directory, write in chunks with delays while keeping handle open, verify daemon does not process mid-write, then close/finish write and verify processing occurs once with complete content.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Only real IO timing reproduces the incomplete-write race this behavior targets.
